violations.text: Inspector observed a live cat and litter box with feces in bathroom.PIC informed to remove cat and litter box along with any feeding supplies from premises. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment, litter, trash, and other debris were observed in the (outside back and surrounding) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/25/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
Cans of food items were observed adulterated by rodent droppings on lids. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
Live cat observed within facility. PIC informed to remove live animal. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by high rodent activity. Inspector observed droppings on retail shelving, on/in food containers, floor throughout retail area, on/in food containers that were stored on the floor in the retail area. Inspector observed nesting materials (diapers/toilet paper) and rodent urine on bags of pasta. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
Drinks, pet food and canned items were observed stored on the floor in the retail area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
Inspector observed previous water damage on several ceiling tiles throughout retail area. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the retail shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025
Inspector observed several ceiling tiles with holes. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2025
The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

The floors, walls, beverage shelves and fans in walk in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023

Inspector observed an accumulation of foliage, beverage containers, an unused trailer surrounding outside area of establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2023
The hand-washing sink in the retail and bathroom area was lacking paper towels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

Food product and beverage containers were observed stored on the: 1.retail area 2.back storage area 3.walk-in cooler 4.bathroom area Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the: 1.retail shelves 2.beverage cooler shelves 3.beverage coolers have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
The walls under the beverage reach in cooler area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The floors, walls, ceilings, and fans in walk in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/02/2023

Inspector observed an accumulation of foliage, litter, and beverage containers surrounding outside area of establishment. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
